title: Create a Contact Form using Widgets

content.problemStatement:
In this practice problem, you have to build a Django form called ContactForm using the some of the widgets you've learned.

#### Task:
Complete the `forms.py` file to create a Django form called `ContactForm`.
The fields to be used and their corresponding types, widgets, labels and attributes that you should use are given below:

| **Field Name**      | **Field Type**      | **Widget**          | **Label**                      | **Attributes (`attrs`)**                        |
| --------------- | --------------- | --------------- | -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| name            | `CharField`     | `TextInput`     | "Your Name"                | `placeholder="Enter your name"`             |
| email           | `EmailField`    | `EmailInput`    | "Your Email"               | `placeholder="Enter your email"`            |
| message         | `CharField`     | `Textarea`      | "Message"                  | `rows=4`, `placeholder="Type your message"` |
| contact\_time   | `DateTimeField` | `DateTimeInput` | "Preferred Contact Time"   | `type="datetime-local"`                     |
| follow\_up      | `BooleanField`  | `CheckboxInput` | "Do you want a follow-up?" | *(No attrs needed)*                         |
| contact\_method | `ChoiceField`   | `RadioSelect`   | "Preferred Contact Method" | *(No attrs needed)*                         |



The views, templates and url mappings are already setup for you. \
After completing the form, click on **RUN** to start the server. \
The expected output is as shown below. Enter some valid and invalid inputs and check if it working properly.
